action.content: Can you write a poem about the pros and cons of playing a lot of video games? Please make sure it's at least 40 sentences long (don't forget to add punctuations). You must highlight some words or phrases in your response, like *highlighted phrase*.
action.label: context

action.content:
The screen glows bright with colors that can **captivate the soul**.
We press a button and a world begins to **unfold before our eyes**.
Heroes rise to fight the dark in battles that are **purely made of light**.
Our fingers dance across the keys with **speed and grace and might**.
It feels like magic when we win a **glorious victory in the fight**.
But time begins to slip away as **hours melt into the night**.
We forget to eat our meals while **levels load and enemies sight**.
The real world fades to gray compared to **vibrant digital delight**.
Our eyes may start to ache from **staring at the glowing light**.
Our backs may hurt from sitting still for **many long and weary nights**.
Social bonds can weaken slowly as we **prefer the virtual fight**.
We miss the laughter of our friends who **wait outside the door**.
We miss the sun that warms our skin and **makes the garden flowers grow**.
The money spent on games can **drain our savings dry**.
We might neglect our studies or our **daily chores and duties**.
Yet stress can vanish in a **fantasy of pure delight**.
Our minds can rest within a **safe and structured fight**.
We learn new skills and strategies that **help us think and write**.
We solve complex puzzles that **challenge our mental might**.
The sense of achievement feels **truly grand and bright**.
We feel powerful when we **defeat the enemy in sight**.
But isolation can creep in when **no one calls our name**.
We might feel lonely in a **crowded digital game**.
The cycle of reward can **become a trap of fate**.
We chase the next high score with **desperate and frantic hate**.
Sleep becomes elusive when the **game calls out our name**.
We dream of dragons and of **castles made of flame**.
But waking up is hard when **dreams feel more like home**.
We trade our health for pixels that **can never truly roam**.
The pros are clear and **undeniably bright and true**.
The cons are shadows that **follow us through and through**.
Balance is the key to **living life with care**.
Too much gaming can **lead us down a stair**.
We must remember that the **real world holds more grace**.
The screen is just a window, not the **entire place of space**.
So put the controller down and **look around you now**.
Feel the wind and hear the **birds singing in a bough**.
The game is fun, but life is **far more deep and true**.
Choose wisely what you play and **what you choose to do**.
Let games be just a **pause in life's grand view**.
Not the whole of everything that **you are meant to do**.
So find the happy middle ground where **joy and health can grow**.
And let the screen be just a **part of who we know**.
action.label: response
